metadata.note: Spec §2 sub-case A2 -- anchor == subject. `Somaliland` is a near-perfect toponym gate: 118 titles/180d, 114 in the FN's centroid scope, ~100% on-topic. Per A2 the bundle is NAME-ONLY: no generic domain verbs, primary_target null, participants widened (AFRICA-HORN + MIDEAST-ISRAEL + USA/EGYPT/TURKEY) because a title cannot carry the name without being on-topic, so wider scope only helps recall. Same shape as arctic greenland_control.
pre_audit_drops: recognition - 116 titles, 70 in scope, but samples are wine-region recognition, Meta facial recognition, Armenian Genocide recognition. Classic A2 generic-verb leak; the participant gate includes ISRAEL and USA so a bare domain noun is unusable here.
secession - 11 titles, 3 in scope, ALL Alberta/Canada separatism.
breakaway - 21 titles, 4 in scope; Netanyahu breakaway party noise. Costs ~2 real Anadolu titles ('Somalia's breakaway region') -- precision over recall.
Berbera - 0 titles/180d despite being the UAE-built port at the centre of the basing story; headlines say 'Somaliland' instead.
صوماليلاند - 0 titles. Arabic corpus uses أرض الصومال (18 titles) instead.
collision_notes: EN matcher is word-START prefix: `Somaliland` cannot be reached by `Somalia` (diverges at position 7), so the sibling atomic somalia_state_security does not swallow this one. The reverse is NOT true -- a bare `Somali` alias would capture all 118 of these titles, so it is banned from the sibling bundle.
